page.title: Sotogrande Yacht Charter During Polo Season: A Luxury Combo

data.meta_description: Book a sotogrande yacht charter during polo season for the ultimate luxury combo. Depart Puerto Banús on a Mangusta 80 from €4,719 with jet ski included.
data.summary: Combine a Sotogrande yacht charter with polo season for a luxury day out. Our fleet departs Puerto Banús, with Tier B boats including a free jet ski from €4,719 for 4 hours.
key_facts: Polo season in Sotogrande runs April to August, peak July and August.
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80, white/grey) start at €4,719 for 4 hours, jet ski included.
Sotogrande is 20 NM south of Puerto Banús, about 45 minutes by powerboat.
Combine a match at Santa María Polo Club with lunch at a beach club in Sotogrande.
Our fleet ranges from 6.4m speedboats (5 pax) to 29m Ferretti 94 (12 pax).
All boats depart from Puerto Banús; no direct Sotogrande departure available.

faq.q: How far is Sotogrande from Puerto Banús by boat?
faq.a: Sotogrande is roughly 20 nautical miles south of Puerto Banús. At cruising speed on a Mangusta 80 (around 22–25 knots), the journey takes about 45 minutes. On a smaller boat like the Astondoa 40, allow closer to an hour. The route hugs the coast, passing Estepona and the Guadiaro river mouth.

faq.q: What boats are best for a Sotogrande yacht charter during polo season?
faq.a: For a group up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (including white/grey variants) is ideal—€4,719 for 4 hours with a free jet ski. The Azimut 58 Flybridge or Ferretti 94 offer more deck space for entertaining. Speedboats like the Mariah SX21 are better for a quick transfer but less suited for lounging.

faq.q: Can we watch polo from the boat in Sotogrande?
faq.a: The Santa María Polo Club is on land, not directly accessible by boat. However, you can anchor near the Guadiaro river mouth, take a tender ashore, and walk to the club. Alternatively, watch from the boat with binoculars as some pitches are visible from the coast. Most guests combine a match with a lunch stop at a beach club.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price for Tier B boats?
faq.a: Tier B boats (Mangusta 80, grey and white) include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, Spanish IVA (21%), and a free jet ski. The price is €4,719 for a minimum 4-hour charter. Longer durations are available on request.

faq.q: Do you offer shorter charters for polo season?
faq.a: Tier B boats require a minimum 4 hours at €4,719, which works well for a half-day Sotogrande trip. For shorter outings, consider Tier A boats like the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 from €749 for 2 hours. These can still reach Sotogrande but with less time to spare.

faq.q: Where can we dock or anchor in Sotogrande?
faq.a: Sotogrande has a marina with visitor berths, but availability during polo season is limited. We recommend anchoring near the Guadiaro river mouth or off the beach at Playa de Sotogrande. Our skipper will advise on the best spot. For lunch, you can take the tender to a beach club like La Reserva or the Sotogrande Marina.

faq.q: What is the best time of day for a polo-season charter?
faq.a: Polo matches typically run from late afternoon into the evening, so a midday departure from Puerto Banús works well. Arrive in Sotogrande around 13:00 for lunch, watch a match from 16:00, then cruise back as the sun sets. This also avoids the midday heat on the water.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food and drinks on board?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and drinks. Our standard inclusions cover soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, and light snacks. If you want a catered lunch or a specific menu, let us know in advance; we can arrange delivery to the boat in Puerto Banús before departure.

data.body_html:
Combine the adrenaline of top-tier polo with the calm of a Mediterranean cruise: a sotogrande yacht charter during polo season is the ultimate luxury combo for Marbella visitors. Depart from Puerto Banús on a Mangusta 80, Azimut 58, or another premium vessel, and within 45 minutes you are anchored off Sotogrande, ready for a match at the Santa María Polo Club or lunch at a beach club. Our all-inclusive pricing—skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and 21% IVA—means no surprises. Here is everything you need to plan your day.

Why Sotogrande for Polo Season

Sotogrande, 20 nautical miles south of Puerto Banús, is Spain's polo capital. The Santa María Polo Club hosts international tournaments from April to August, with peak season in July and August. The atmosphere blends equestrian elegance with Andalusian glamour. A boat rental Marbella gives you the flexibility to watch a match, enjoy a long lunch at a beach club like La Reserva, and cruise back along the coast as the sun sets over La Concha.

Our Fleet for the Trip

We operate from Puerto Banús only—no direct Sotogrande departures—but our fleet covers every group size and budget. For a polo day, Tier B boats are the sweet spot:

  • Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax, from €4,719 for 4 hours, jet ski included)
  • Mangusta 80 Grey (24m, 12 pax, same pricing)
  • Mangusta 80 White (24m, 12 pax, same pricing)
  • Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax, pricing on request)
  • Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax, pricing on request)

Tier A boats like the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) or Azimut 39 (€749/2h) work for smaller groups or shorter trips, but the 20 NM run means less time in Sotogrande. For a full day, the 8-hour Tier A option (€2,299) gives you 6+ hours on site.

What's Included

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Tier B boats add a free jet ski. No hidden costs. You can bring your own food or arrange catering in advance—just let us know.

Sample Itinerary: Polo Day from Puerto Banús

  1. 11:30 – Board your boat at Puerto Banús. Meet the skipper, stow bags, and enjoy a welcome drink.
  2. 12:00 – Depart south, passing the Marbella coastline, Estepona, and the Sierra Bermeja foothills.
  3. 12:45 – Anchor off Sotogrande. Take the tender ashore for lunch at a beach club or marina restaurant.
  4. 15:30 – Head to Santa María Polo Club for the match (tickets not included; book directly).
  5. 18:00 – Return to the boat for a sunset cruise back to Puerto Banús.
  6. 19:00 – Arrive at Puerto Banús.

This itinerary works perfectly with a 4-hour Tier B charter (€4,719) or an 8-hour Tier A charter (€2,299). Adjust timings to suit the match schedule.

Pricing Overview

BoatLengthPaxPrice (4h)Jet Ski
Mangusta 80 (all variants)24m12€4,719Free
Astondoa 4012.5m9€1,299 (4h)No
Azimut 3912.5m11€1,299 (4h)No
Other boats (Azimut 58, Ferretti 94, etc.)17.6–29m12On requestOn request

All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and IVA. Tier B minimum is 4 hours; longer durations available via WhatsApp.

Book Your Sotogrande Yacht Charter

Ready to combine luxury boating with top polo? Browse our full fleet and contact us via WhatsApp for availability. Mention 'polo season' for priority scheduling. We recommend booking at least two weeks ahead in July and August.

Frequently Asked Questions

How far is Sotogrande from Puerto Banús by boat?

Sotogrande is roughly 20 nautical miles south of Puerto Banús. At cruising speed on a Mangusta 80 (around 22–25 knots), the journey takes about 45 minutes. On a smaller boat like the Astondoa 40, allow closer to an hour. The route hugs the coast, passing Estepona and the Guadiaro river mouth.

What boats are best for a Sotogrande yacht charter during polo season?

For a group up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (including white/grey variants) is ideal—€4,719 for 4 hours with a free jet ski. The Azimut 58 Flybridge or Ferretti 94 offer more deck space for entertaining. Speedboats like the Mariah SX21 are better for a quick transfer but less suited for lounging.

Can we watch polo from the boat in Sotogrande?

The Santa María Polo Club is on land, not directly accessible by boat. However, you can anchor near the Guadiaro river mouth, take a tender ashore, and walk to the club. Alternatively, watch from the boat with binoculars as some pitches are visible from the coast. Most guests combine a match with a lunch stop at a beach club.

What is included in the charter price for Tier B boats?

Tier B boats (Mangusta 80, grey and white) include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, Spanish IVA (21%), and a free jet ski. The price is €4,719 for a minimum 4-hour charter. Longer durations are available on request.

Do you offer shorter charters for polo season?

Tier B boats require a minimum 4 hours at €4,719, which works well for a half-day Sotogrande trip. For shorter outings, consider Tier A boats like the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 from €749 for 2 hours. These can still reach Sotogrande but with less time to spare.

Where can we dock or anchor in Sotogrande?

Sotogrande has a marina with visitor berths, but availability during polo season is limited. We recommend anchoring near the Guadiaro river mouth or off the beach at Playa de Sotogrande. Our skipper will advise on the best spot. For lunch, you can take the tender to a beach club like La Reserva or the Sotogrande Marina.

What is the best time of day for a polo-season charter?

Polo matches typically run from late afternoon into the evening, so a midday departure from Puerto Banús works well. Arrive in Sotogrande around 13:00 for lunch, watch a match from 16:00, then cruise back as the sun sets. This also avoids the midday heat on the water.

Can we bring our own food and drinks on board?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and drinks. Our standard inclusions cover soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, and light snacks. If you want a catered lunch or a specific menu, let us know in advance; we can arrange delivery to the boat in Puerto Banús before departure.
